findings.description: Header and footer artifacts occasionally interrupt reading flow mid-sentence.
findings.evidence: At the transition from page 3 to page 4, the PDF breaks a sentence ('...was water willow...'). In the HTML, the page number '2' and document title are inserted directly between these words, slightly disrupting the natural reading order.

findings.description: The HTML table structure introduces a new column header ('Scientific Name') that does not explicitly appear in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, Table 1 has three header columns: 'Survey Overview', '#', and '%'. The HTML table adds a fourth header: '<th>Scientific Name</th>' to accommodate the scientific names of the species.

findings.description: The document lacks an <h1> tag, breaking the heading hierarchy by starting directly with <h2> tags.
findings.evidence:
The document title is styled using a generic div ('
Roanoke Rapids Lake Vegetation Survey Report
') and the first heading tag used in the HTML is '

Introduction

'.
